symbols: winged lion — the traditional symbol of the Evangelist Mark
open book — the Gospel of Mark
Venetian lagoon — Venice under the patronage of St. Mark
description: A painting of the winged Lion of Saint Mark, Venice's symbol, standing with a paw on an open book inscribed with the traditional words 'PAX TIBI MARCE.' The lion has large wings and a halo. The Venetian lagoon and city are visible in the background.
theological_description: The winged lion is the traditional symbol of the Evangelist Mark, derived from Ezekiel's vision of four living creatures (EZK.1.10) and Revelation's four creatures around the throne (REV.4.7). Mark's Gospel begins with John the Baptist crying in the wilderness like a lion's roar, which tradition linked to the lion symbol. Mark's Gospel emphasizes Christ's power and action — the kingly lion. Venice adopted St. Mark as patron after his relics were translated there in 828 AD.

scripture_refs.reason: The four living creatures, the second like a lion — Mark's evangelist symbol
